name: Controller Offline
description: An E-Series controller is offline and not responding to management requests
check_query: SELECT array_name, controller_label, status, partner_status, last_contact FROM eseries_controllers WHERE status = 'offline'
condition: Controller status is offline
impact: Loss of controller redundancy; if partner controller fails, complete array outage; all volumes on affected controller must failover to partner
remediation_steps: 1. SANtricity System Manager > Hardware > Controller shelf view. 2. Check controller status LEDs (amber = fault). 3. Verify controller has not been physically removed or reseated. 4. Check management network connectivity to controller. 5. Review MEL (Major Event Log) for error events: SANtricity > Support > Event Log. 6. Attempt controller reset via SANtricity if accessible. 7. Verify partner controller has assumed ownership of all volumes. 8. If hardware failure confirmed, contact NetApp for replacement controller
support_case_note: Open Severity 1 case immediately for controller offline. Provide array serial number and MEL event codes. NetApp will dispatch replacement controller if hardware failure confirmed

name: Controller in Service Mode
description: An E-Series controller is in service mode and not serving I/O
check_query: SELECT array_name, controller_label, status, service_mode_reason, partner_status FROM eseries_controllers WHERE status = 'serviceMode'
condition: Controller is in service mode
impact: Controller not serving I/O; all volumes running on partner controller; reduced redundancy; increased latency due to single-controller operation
remediation_steps: 1. SANtricity System Manager > Hardware > check controller status. 2. Determine why controller entered service mode (firmware update, manual action, error recovery). 3. If service mode was intentional (firmware update), wait for completion and verify return to optimal. 4. If unintentional, review MEL for root cause. 5. Attempt to bring controller online: SANtricity > Hardware > select controller > Place Online. 6. Verify all volumes return to preferred controller ownership after recovery
support_case_note: Open support case if controller will not exit service mode or repeatedly enters service mode unexpectedly

name: Drive Failed
description: One or more drives in the storage array have failed
check_query: SELECT array_name, drive_location, drive_type, pool_name, status, failure_reason FROM eseries_drives WHERE status = 'failed'
condition: One or more drives report failed status
impact: Data redundancy reduced for affected pool/volume group; reconstruction onto hot spare consuming I/O bandwidth; risk of data loss if additional drives fail before reconstruction completes
remediation_steps: 1. SANtricity System Manager > Hardware > locate failed drive (amber LED). 2. Note drive tray/slot location for physical identification. 3. Check if hot spare has been automatically assigned and reconstruction started. 4. Monitor reconstruction progress: SANtricity > Pools & Volume Groups > select affected pool. 5. Do NOT remove the failed drive until replacement is available. 6. Contact NetApp for replacement drive dispatch. 7. When replacement arrives, physically replace the failed drive. 8. Verify reconstruction starts on new drive. 9. If no hot spare available, replacement drive becomes reconstruction target
support_case_note: Open Severity 1 case for drive failure. Provide array serial number, drive tray/slot, and drive part number. NetApp will dispatch replacement drive. If multiple drives failed simultaneously, escalate to Severity 1 critical

name: Drive Predictive Failure
description: A drive is reporting SMART predictive failure indicators, suggesting imminent drive failure
check_query: SELECT array_name, drive_location, drive_type, pool_name, smart_status, predicted_failure_date FROM eseries_drives WHERE smart_status = 'predictive_failure'
condition: Drive SMART status reports predictive failure
impact: Drive may fail at any time; if drive fails, reconstruction will consume I/O bandwidth; potential data loss risk if multiple drives affected
remediation_steps: 1. SANtricity System Manager > Hardware > identify drive with predictive failure indicator. 2. Note drive location (tray/slot) and part number. 3. Proactively replace the drive during next maintenance window. 4. If hot spare available, initiate proactive copy-back: SANtricity > Hardware > select drive > Replace Drive. 5. Contact NetApp for replacement drive. 6. Schedule replacement during low-I/O period to minimize performance impact. 7. After replacement, verify new drive integrates successfully into pool
support_case_note: Open Severity 3 proactive case for replacement drive dispatch. Provide drive serial number and location

name: Power Supply Failed
description: A power supply unit in a controller shelf or drive shelf has failed
check_query: SELECT array_name, shelf_id, psu_location, status, shelf_type FROM eseries_power_supplies WHERE status = 'failed'
condition: Power supply status is failed
impact: Loss of power redundancy for the affected shelf; if remaining PSU fails, shelf will lose power causing complete outage for all components in that shelf
remediation_steps: 1. SANtricity System Manager > Hardware > identify affected shelf. 2. Check PSU status LEDs on the shelf (amber = fault). 3. Verify power cables and power source for the failed PSU. 4. Check if power is being supplied from redundant circuit. 5. Do NOT remove the failed PSU if it has a fan integrated — loss of cooling may occur. 6. Contact NetApp for replacement PSU. 7. Replace PSU as hot-swap operation (no downtime required for redundant PSU). 8. Verify power supply status returns to optimal after replacement
support_case_note: Open Severity 1 case for PSU failure. Provide array serial, shelf ID, and PSU location. NetApp will dispatch replacement

name: Fan Failed
description: A cooling fan in a controller shelf or drive shelf has failed
check_query: SELECT array_name, shelf_id, fan_location, status, shelf_type FROM eseries_fans WHERE status = 'failed'
condition: Fan status is failed
impact: Reduced cooling capacity for the affected shelf; thermal protection may shut down components if temperature exceeds thresholds; risk of cascading hardware failures due to overheating
remediation_steps: 1. SANtricity System Manager > Hardware > identify affected shelf and fan. 2. Check shelf temperature indicators for thermal warnings. 3. Ensure data center cooling is adequate and not blocked near the affected shelf. 4. If fan is part of a fan canister, the entire canister must be replaced. 5. Contact NetApp for replacement fan/canister. 6. Replace as hot-swap operation. 7. Monitor temperatures after replacement. 8. Verify status returns to optimal in SANtricity
support_case_note: Open Severity 1 case for fan failure. Provide array serial, shelf ID, and fan location. Urgent if temperatures are rising

name: Battery Failed or Missing
description: A controller cache battery has failed, is missing, or is not charging
check_query: SELECT array_name, controller_label, battery_status, battery_age_days, charge_level_pct FROM eseries_batteries WHERE battery_status NOT IN ('optimal', 'charging')
condition: Battery status is not optimal or charging
impact: Write cache disabled on affected controller; significant write performance degradation; data in cache at risk during power loss; controller may force write-through mode
remediation_steps: 1. SANtricity System Manager > Hardware > Controller > Battery status. 2. Verify battery status (failed, expired, missing, not charging). 3. Check battery age — batteries have a finite lifespan (typically 2-3 years). 4. If battery is replaceable, schedule replacement during maintenance window. 5. Controller cache will be disabled until battery is replaced. 6. Contact NetApp for replacement battery. 7. After replacement, monitor battery learn cycle and charging status. 8. Verify write cache is re-enabled after battery reaches optimal charge
support_case_note: Open Severity 2 case for battery failure. Write caching is disabled, causing performance impact. Provide controller serial and battery status

name: Drive Reconstruction in Progress
description: A drive reconstruction operation is currently in progress after a drive failure or replacement
check_query: SELECT array_name, drive_location, pool_name, reconstruction_pct, estimated_completion, reconstruction_priority FROM eseries_reconstruction WHERE reconstruction_pct < 100
condition: Drive reconstruction is in progress
impact: Reduced data protection until reconstruction completes; additional drive failure during reconstruction may cause data loss; I/O performance reduced due to reconstruction overhead
remediation_steps: 1. SANtricity System Manager > Pools & Volume Groups > check reconstruction progress. 2. Monitor reconstruction percentage and estimated time to completion. 3. Consider adjusting reconstruction priority: SANtricity > Settings > System > Set reconstruction priority to High if data protection is priority over performance. 4. Do NOT perform any additional maintenance operations during reconstruction. 5. Avoid removing or adding drives during reconstruction. 6. Monitor I/O latency — alert application teams if performance impact is significant. 7. After completion, verify pool returns to optimal status
support_case_note: Open support case if reconstruction stalls (no progress for >4 hours) or if reconstruction percentage decreases

name: Pool Capacity Warning
description: Storage pool utilization has exceeded 85% of usable capacity
check_query: SELECT array_name, pool_name, used_capacity_pct, total_capacity_tb, available_capacity_tb, volume_count FROM eseries_pools WHERE used_capacity_pct > 85 AND used_capacity_pct <= 95
condition: Pool capacity utilization exceeds 85%
impact: Approaching pool capacity limits; thin-provisioned volumes may fail to allocate space; write performance may degrade as pool fills
remediation_steps: 1. SANtricity System Manager > Pools & Volume Groups > select pool. 2. Review allocated vs. available capacity. 3. Identify volumes that can be deleted or reduced in size. 4. Check for orphaned volumes not attached to any host. 5. Review thin-provisioned volume over-allocation ratio. 6. Plan capacity expansion by adding drives to the pool. 7. If DDP pool: additional drives can be added non-disruptively. 8. For RAID groups: plan additional volume group creation with new drives
support_case_note: Open support case for capacity planning assistance or guidance on pool expansion options

name: Pool Capacity Critical
description: Storage pool utilization has exceeded 95% and is at imminent risk of exhaustion
check_query: SELECT array_name, pool_name, used_capacity_pct, total_capacity_tb, available_capacity_tb, thin_provisioned_alert FROM eseries_pools WHERE used_capacity_pct > 95
condition: Pool capacity utilization exceeds 95%
impact: Imminent capacity exhaustion; thin-provisioned volumes will fail writes when pool is full; host I/O errors; potential application outage
remediation_steps: 1. SANtricity System Manager > Pools & Volume Groups > assess remaining capacity. 2. URGENT: Identify and delete any unnecessary volumes immediately. 3. Check for snapshots consuming pool capacity and delete unneeded snapshots. 4. Increase thin-provisioned volume repository capacity if needed. 5. Add drives to pool immediately if available: SANtricity > Pools > Add Capacity. 6. Contact NetApp for emergency drive procurement. 7. Consider temporarily migrating workloads to other pools with available capacity. 8. Disable snapshot schedules temporarily to prevent additional space consumption
support_case_note: Open Severity 1 case if pool is full and host writes are failing. Open Severity 2 for urgent capacity expansion

name: Thin Volume Repository Full
description: A thin-provisioned volume's repository is at or near capacity, risking write failures
check_query: SELECT array_name, volume_name, repository_used_pct, repository_total_gb, repository_available_gb, auto_expand_enabled FROM eseries_thin_volumes WHERE repository_used_pct > 90
condition: Thin volume repository utilization exceeds 90%
impact: Writes to this volume will fail when repository is full; host I/O errors; application outage for affected workloads
remediation_steps: 1. SANtricity System Manager > Volumes > select thin volume > Repository details. 2. Check if auto-expansion is enabled — if not, enable it. 3. Verify pool has sufficient free capacity for repository expansion. 4. Manually expand repository capacity if needed: SANtricity > Volumes > Increase Capacity. 5. Set appropriate warning and critical thresholds for repository utilization. 6. Review application data patterns — high write amplification may cause rapid repository growth. 7. Consider converting to thick-provisioned volume if repository management is problematic
support_case_note: Open Severity 1 case if thin volume repository is full and host writes are failing. Open Severity 2 if auto-expand cannot allocate space from pool

name: Snapshot Repository Capacity Warning
description: Snapshot repository capacity is approaching its limit, risking snapshot operation failures
check_query: SELECT array_name, snapshot_group_name, base_volume_name, repository_used_pct, repository_total_gb, auto_delete_enabled FROM eseries_snapshot_groups WHERE repository_used_pct > 80
condition: Snapshot repository utilization exceeds 80%
impact: When repository is full, oldest snapshots are auto-deleted (if enabled) or new snapshots fail; potential loss of recovery points
remediation_steps: 1. SANtricity System Manager > Volumes > Snapshot Groups > select group. 2. Review repository utilization and snapshot count. 3. Delete unneeded snapshots to free repository space. 4. If auto-delete is enabled, verify policy is acceptable for recovery point objectives. 5. Increase snapshot repository capacity if needed: add additional repository volume capacity. 6. Review snapshot scheduling — reduce frequency if creating excessive snapshots. 7. Consider snapshot consistency group settings for multi-volume workloads
support_case_note: Open support case if snapshot operations are failing despite available pool capacity

name: Hot Spare Insufficient
description: Insufficient hot spare drives available to cover drive failures in configured pools
check_query: SELECT array_name, hot_spare_count, recommended_hot_spares, pool_count, drive_type, drive_capacity FROM eseries_hot_spares WHERE hot_spare_count < recommended_hot_spares
condition: Hot spare count is less than recommended count for the array configuration
impact: If a drive fails, no hot spare available for automatic reconstruction; must wait for replacement drive from NetApp; extended data protection gap; manual intervention required
remediation_steps: 1. SANtricity System Manager > Hardware > review unassigned drives. 2. Assign unassigned drives as hot spares: Hardware > select drive > Assign as Hot Spare. 3. Ensure hot spare drives match the type and capacity of pool drives (SSD for SSD pools, HDD for HDD pools). 4. NetApp recommends at least 1 hot spare per 30 drives, minimum 1 per drive type. 5. If no unassigned drives available, plan procurement of additional drives. 6. For DDP pools, built-in protection reduces hot spare urgency but spare is still recommended. 7. Review hot spare distribution across shelves for fault domain coverage
support_case_note: Open support case for best practice guidance on hot spare allocation, or to order additional drives

name: Volume Latency Exceeds Threshold
description: Volume I/O latency is consistently exceeding 20ms, indicating a performance bottleneck
check_query: SELECT array_name, volume_name, avg_read_latency_ms, avg_write_latency_ms, iops, throughput_mbps FROM eseries_volume_performance WHERE (avg_read_latency_ms > 20 OR avg_write_latency_ms > 20) AND sample_duration_minutes > 15
condition: Volume read or write latency exceeds 20ms for more than 15 minutes
impact: Application performance degradation; database query times increased; user experience impacted; potential SLA breaches for latency-sensitive workloads
remediation_steps: 1. SANtricity System Manager > Performance > select volume for performance graphs. 2. Identify if latency is read-dominant or write-dominant. 3. For write latency: Check cache hit ratio — low cache hit ratio suggests cache thrashing. 4. Verify write cache is enabled (requires functioning battery). 5. Check for hot volumes on same controller — rebalance ownership. 6. Review workload I/O patterns (random vs. sequential, block size). 7. Consider SSD caching (SSD Read Cache) for read-heavy workloads on HDD pools. 8. If using Dynamic Disk Pools, verify optimal drive count and distribution. 9. Consider migrating to SSD pool for latency-sensitive workloads
support_case_note: Open support case if latency is high with no identifiable cause, or if performance degradation began after a firmware update

name: Controller CPU Utilization High
description: E-Series controller CPU utilization is consistently above 85%
check_query: SELECT array_name, controller_label, cpu_utilization_pct, iops, throughput_mbps, cache_hit_pct FROM eseries_controller_performance WHERE cpu_utilization_pct > 85 AND sample_duration_minutes > 15
condition: Controller CPU utilization exceeds 85% for more than 15 minutes
impact: I/O processing delays; increased latency across all volumes on this controller; request queuing; potential I/O timeouts
remediation_steps: 1. SANtricity System Manager > Performance > Controller view. 2. Identify IOPS and throughput levels — is workload within specifications? 3. Check volume ownership balance between controllers — rebalance if skewed. 4. Review read cache hit ratio — low ratios increase CPU load. 5. Check for background operations consuming CPU (reconstruction, copyback, consistency check). 6. If persistently high, consider workload redistribution or platform upgrade. 7. Review volume access patterns — excessive small I/O increases CPU overhead. 8. Check for host-side multipath settings that may cause excessive retries
support_case_note: Open support case if controller CPU is consistently maxed with workload within documented specifications

name: Cache Hit Ratio Below Threshold
description: Controller cache hit ratio is below 50%, indicating inefficient use of cache resources
check_query: SELECT array_name, controller_label, read_cache_hit_pct, write_cache_hit_pct, cache_size_gb FROM eseries_controller_performance WHERE read_cache_hit_pct < 50 AND sample_duration_minutes > 30
condition: Read cache hit ratio is below 50% for more than 30 minutes
impact: More I/O served from slower disk; increased latency; higher CPU utilization; reduced overall array performance
remediation_steps: 1. SANtricity System Manager > Performance > check cache statistics. 2. Review workload I/O patterns — fully random I/O may not benefit from caching. 3. Check cache settings per volume: SANtricity > Volumes > select volume > Cache Settings. 4. Enable read prefetch for sequential workloads. 5. Verify write caching is enabled (requires healthy battery). 6. Consider enabling SSD Read Cache: SANtricity > Pools > SSD Cache. 7. Ensure cache is not being wasted on volumes with fully random I/O patterns. 8. Review volume segment size — align with application I/O size
support_case_note: Open support case for cache tuning assistance or if cache hit ratio dropped significantly after a firmware update

name: Write Cache Disabled
description: Write caching is disabled on a volume, resulting in degraded write performance
check_query: SELECT array_name, volume_name, write_cache_enabled, write_cache_mirroring, battery_status FROM eseries_volumes WHERE write_cache_enabled = false
condition: Write caching is disabled on a volume
impact: All writes go directly to disk; significant write latency increase (10-100x); application write performance severely degraded; may cause timeouts for write-intensive workloads
remediation_steps: 1. SANtricity System Manager > Volumes > select volume > Change Cache Settings. 2. If write cache was manually disabled, re-enable it. 3. Check battery status — write cache is automatically disabled if battery is failed/missing. 4. If battery issue, see rule es-avail-007 for battery replacement. 5. Check if write cache mirroring is causing the disable (both controllers must have healthy batteries). 6. After enabling, monitor write performance improvement. 7. If write cache was disabled due to data integrity concerns, investigate root cause before re-enabling
support_case_note: Open support case if write cache cannot be re-enabled or is being automatically disabled repeatedly

name: No Snapshot Schedules Configured
description: No snapshot schedules are configured for volumes in the array, limiting local data protection
check_query: SELECT array_name, volume_count, volumes_with_snapshots, snapshot_schedule_count FROM eseries_snapshot_summary WHERE snapshot_schedule_count = 0 AND volume_count > 0
condition: No snapshot schedules exist on an array with active volumes
impact: No local point-in-time recovery capability; accidental data corruption or deletion cannot be recovered from snapshots; longer RTO for recovery scenarios
remediation_steps: 1. SANtricity System Manager > Volumes > Snapshots. 2. Create snapshot groups for critical volumes. 3. Configure snapshot schedules: SANtricity > Volumes > Snapshot Schedules > Create. 4. Set schedule frequency based on RPO requirements (e.g., hourly, every 4 hours). 5. Configure snapshot repository capacity (start with 20% of base volume). 6. Enable auto-delete policy with appropriate oldest-first deletion. 7. Consider consistency groups for multi-volume workloads (e.g., databases). 8. Test snapshot recovery procedure to validate data protection
support_case_note: Not required. Open support case for best practice guidance on snapshot strategies for specific workloads

name: Async Mirror Group Unsynchronized
description: An asynchronous mirror group is not synchronized, indicating replication lag to the secondary array
check_query: SELECT array_name, mirror_group_name, sync_status, last_sync_time, pending_writes_mb, remote_array_name FROM eseries_async_mirrors WHERE sync_status != 'synchronized'
condition: Async mirror group is not in synchronized status
impact: DR site not current; potential data loss if primary array fails; RPO target not being met; compliance risk for data replication requirements
remediation_steps: 1. SANtricity System Manager > Storage > Asynchronous Mirroring. 2. Check mirror group status and last successful synchronization time. 3. Verify connectivity to secondary/remote array. 4. Check bandwidth between sites — may need QoS or dedicated replication link. 5. Review pending write volume — large backlogs indicate sustained bandwidth shortfall. 6. Manually initiate synchronization if auto-sync has stalled. 7. Check for volume capacity issues on the secondary array. 8. Review synchronization interval settings — may need adjustment for data change rate
support_case_note: Open support case if mirror group will not synchronize after verifying connectivity and capacity

name: Sync Mirror Unsynchronized
description: A synchronous mirror pair is not synchronized, meaning zero-RPO replication has been broken
check_query: SELECT array_name, volume_name, mirror_status, role, remote_volume, remote_array FROM eseries_sync_mirrors WHERE mirror_status != 'optimal'
condition: Synchronous mirror pair is not in optimal status
impact: Zero-RPO replication broken; data on secondary is not guaranteed current; if primary fails, potential data loss; SLA violation for synchronous replication requirements
remediation_steps: 1. SANtricity System Manager > Storage > Synchronous Mirroring. 2. Check mirror pair status and identify desynchronization cause. 3. Verify inter-array connectivity (FC or iSCSI replication link). 4. Check for link latency — synchronous mirroring requires <5ms round-trip. 5. Verify both volumes are accessible and not in error state. 6. Manually re-synchronize if needed: SANtricity > re-synchronize pair. 7. Full synchronization will be required if delta log has overflowed. 8. Monitor synchronization progress and verify optimal status after completion
support_case_note: Open Severity 1 case if synchronous mirror is broken and cannot be re-established, especially if zero-RPO is a compliance requirement

name: Data Assurance (DA) Errors Detected
description: Data Assurance has detected data integrity errors in I/O paths
check_query: SELECT array_name, volume_name, da_error_count, last_da_error, error_type FROM eseries_da_errors WHERE da_error_count > 0
condition: Data Assurance error count is greater than zero
impact: Potential data corruption detected and prevented; indicates hardware or firmware issue in I/O path; may affect data integrity if DA is not enabled on all volumes
remediation_steps: 1. SANtricity System Manager > Support > Event Log > filter for DA errors. 2. Identify affected volumes and drives. 3. Check for drive errors that may be causing data integrity issues. 4. Verify SAS/FC cable integrity between controllers and drive shelves. 5. Check controller and drive shelf IOM firmware versions. 6. Run consistency check on affected volumes: SANtricity > Pools > Run Consistency Check. 7. If errors persist, suspect hardware issue in shelf interconnect. 8. Collect support bundle and open support case
support_case_note: Always open support case for DA errors — they indicate potential data integrity issues requiring investigation

name: Management Certificate Expiring
description: The SANtricity management interface TLS certificate will expire within 30 days
check_query: SELECT array_name, cert_type, expiry_date, days_until_expiry, issued_to, issuer FROM eseries_certificates WHERE days_until_expiry BETWEEN 1 AND 30
condition: Management certificate expires within 30 days
impact: When certificate expires, browser warnings will appear; management API calls may fail; monitoring integrations that validate certificates will break; security compliance violation
remediation_steps: 1. SANtricity System Manager > Settings > Certificates. 2. Review certificate expiry date and type (self-signed or CA-signed). 3. For CA-signed: Generate CSR from SANtricity, submit to CA, install renewed certificate. 4. For self-signed: Regenerate via SANtricity Settings > Certificates > Reset. 5. After renewal, verify management access works with new certificate. 6. Update any monitoring tools or scripts that pin or validate the certificate. 7. Set calendar reminder for next renewal
support_case_note: Open support case only if certificate renewal causes management access issues or if SANtricity certificate management interface is inaccessible

name: Drive Security Not Enabled
description: Full Disk Encryption (FDE) or FIPS drive security is not enabled on pools containing sensitive data
check_query: SELECT array_name, pool_name, security_enabled, drive_security_capable, key_management_type FROM eseries_pools WHERE security_enabled = false AND drive_security_capable = true
condition: Security-capable drives are not using drive security
impact: Data at rest is not encrypted; physical theft of drives could expose data; regulatory non-compliance for environments requiring encryption (HIPAA, PCI-DSS, GDPR)
remediation_steps: 1. SANtricity System Manager > Settings > System > Security Key Management. 2. Create or import a security key (local key management or external KMIP server). 3. For external key management, configure KMIP server connection. 4. Enable security on pools: SANtricity > Pools > select pool > Enable Security. 5. Note: Enabling security is non-disruptive but cannot be reversed without data loss. 6. Test drive security by ensuring locked drives cannot be read in another array. 7. CRITICAL: Securely back up the security key — loss of key means data is permanently inaccessible
support_case_note: Open support case for guidance on drive security implementation or KMIP integration. Contact support IMMEDIATELY if security key is lost

name: Default Password Not Changed
description: SANtricity management interface is still using default or initial passwords
check_query: SELECT array_name, admin_password_age_days, monitor_password_set, last_password_change FROM eseries_security_config WHERE admin_password_age_days IS NULL OR admin_password_age_days > 365
condition: Admin password has not been changed or is over 365 days old
impact: Unauthorized access to storage management; configuration changes by unauthorized personnel; data exposure; audit non-compliance
remediation_steps: 1. SANtricity System Manager > Settings > Access Management. 2. Change the admin password to a strong, unique password. 3. Set the monitor (read-only) password if not already configured. 4. Configure LDAP/AD integration for centralized authentication. 5. Document new credentials in organization's password vault. 6. Review and configure role-based access control. 7. Enable audit logging for management access. 8. Consider disabling local admin account after LDAP integration is verified
support_case_note: Open support case only if locked out of management interface

name: SANtricity OS Not Current
description: The SANtricity OS version on the E-Series controller is not the latest generally available release
check_query: SELECT array_name, current_version, latest_version, version_age_days FROM eseries_firmware WHERE firmware_type = 'controller_os' AND current_version != latest_version
condition: SANtricity OS version does not match latest available version
impact: Missing security patches, bug fixes, and performance improvements; may lack features required by newer applications or management tools
remediation_steps: 1. Download SANtricity OS from mysupport.netapp.com. 2. Review release notes for upgrade path and known issues. 3. SANtricity System Manager > Upgrade Center > Software and Firmware > SANtricity OS. 4. Upload SANtricity OS package. 5. Run pre-upgrade health check. 6. Schedule maintenance window — upgrade performs rolling controller restart (one at a time). 7. I/O continues during upgrade but with reduced redundancy during each controller restart. 8. After upgrade, verify both controllers are running new version. 9. Check for post-upgrade configuration adjustments noted in release notes
support_case_note: Open support case for upgrade planning, especially for major version upgrades. Contact support if upgrade fails or controller does not come back online

name: Controller NVSRAM Outdated
description: Controller NVSRAM is not at the recommended version for the current SANtricity OS
check_query: SELECT array_name, controller_label, nvsram_version, recommended_nvsram, santricity_version FROM eseries_firmware WHERE firmware_type = 'nvsram' AND nvsram_version != recommended_nvsram
condition: NVSRAM version does not match recommended version for current SANtricity OS
impact: Suboptimal controller configuration; may not have correct default settings for controller hardware; performance may be affected
remediation_steps: 1. Download recommended NVSRAM from mysupport.netapp.com (matched to SANtricity OS version). 2. SANtricity System Manager > Upgrade Center > Software and Firmware > NVSRAM. 3. Upload and apply NVSRAM file. 4. NVSRAM update typically included with SANtricity OS upgrade. 5. If updating separately, plan for brief controller restart. 6. Verify NVSRAM version after update
support_case_note: Open support case if unsure which NVSRAM version matches your SANtricity OS, or if NVSRAM update fails

name: Drive Firmware Outdated
description: One or more drives are running firmware that is not at the latest recommended version
check_query: SELECT array_name, drive_location, drive_model, current_firmware, latest_firmware, drive_type FROM eseries_firmware WHERE firmware_type = 'drive' AND current_firmware != latest_firmware
condition: Drive firmware does not match latest recommended version
impact: Missing drive-level bug fixes and reliability improvements; potential compatibility issues with newer SANtricity OS versions
remediation_steps: 1. Download drive firmware from mysupport.netapp.com. 2. SANtricity System Manager > Upgrade Center > Drive Firmware. 3. Upload drive firmware package. 4. Select drives to update (can select all matching drives). 5. For DDP pools: drive firmware update is non-disruptive (online). 6. For RAID volume groups: may require scheduling based on RAID level and spare availability. 7. Monitor update progress — drives are updated sequentially. 8. Verify firmware version on all drives after update completes
support_case_note: Open support case if drive firmware update fails or if drives become unresponsive after update

name: IOM/ESM Firmware Outdated
description: Input/Output Module (IOM) or Environmental Services Module (ESM) firmware in drive shelves is outdated
check_query: SELECT array_name, shelf_id, iom_label, current_firmware, latest_firmware FROM eseries_firmware WHERE firmware_type = 'iom' AND current_firmware != latest_firmware
condition: IOM/ESM firmware does not match latest recommended version
impact: Potential shelf connectivity issues; missing bug fixes for SAS path management; may affect drive error recovery; compatibility risk with newer SANtricity versions
remediation_steps: 1. Download IOM/ESM firmware from mysupport.netapp.com. 2. SANtricity System Manager > Upgrade Center > check for IOM firmware updates. 3. IOM updates are typically performed via SANtricity during SANtricity OS upgrade. 4. For manual update, follow NetApp documentation for the specific shelf model. 5. IOM update is typically non-disruptive with dual-path connectivity. 6. Verify both IOMs in each shelf are updated. 7. Check SAS path redundancy after update
support_case_note: Open support case if IOM firmware update fails or if shelf connectivity issues occur after update

name: SANtricity OS End of Support
description: The SANtricity OS version has reached or is approaching end of support lifecycle
check_query: SELECT array_name, current_version, eol_date, days_until_eol, support_status FROM eseries_firmware WHERE firmware_type = 'controller_os' AND eol_date IS NOT NULL AND days_until_eol < 180
condition: SANtricity OS version EOL within 180 days or already EOL
impact: No security patches or critical bug fixes; limited NetApp Support ability; compliance risk; insurance/liability implications for regulated environments
remediation_steps: 1. Plan upgrade to a supported SANtricity OS version immediately. 2. Review NetApp Interoperability Matrix Tool (IMT) for upgrade path. 3. Download target version from mysupport.netapp.com. 4. Open proactive support case for upgrade planning. 5. Submit RFC to CAB with EOL urgency justification. 6. Plan and execute upgrade during approved maintenance window. 7. Verify all components (controllers, drives, IOMs) are compatible with target version. 8. Test all host connectivity and application functionality after upgrade
support_case_note: Open Severity 3 proactive case for upgrade planning. If already past EOL, escalate urgency with NetApp Account Team

name: Volume Ownership Imbalanced
description: Volume ownership is significantly imbalanced between controllers, causing uneven workload distribution
check_query: SELECT array_name, controller_a_volume_count, controller_b_volume_count, imbalance_pct FROM eseries_volume_balance WHERE imbalance_pct > 30
condition: Volume ownership imbalance exceeds 30% between controllers
impact: One controller handling disproportionate workload; higher latency on overloaded controller; underutilized capacity on partner controller; reduced effective array performance
remediation_steps: 1. SANtricity System Manager > Volumes > review volume ownership (Controller A vs. B). 2. Identify non-preferred volume ownership (volumes running on non-preferred controller). 3. Redistribute volumes: SANtricity > Volumes > Change Ownership (select preferred controller). 4. Volume ownership changes are non-disruptive but cause brief I/O pause during transfer. 5. Balance based on IOPS/throughput, not just volume count. 6. Schedule ownership changes during low-I/O periods for minimal impact. 7. Enable automatic load balancing if available on your SANtricity version
support_case_note: Open support case for workload balancing best practices or if automatic load balancing is not functioning as expected

name: Host Connectivity Not Multipathed
description: A host is connected to the array via a single path, without multipath redundancy
check_query: SELECT array_name, host_name, path_count, host_type, connected_ports FROM eseries_host_connectivity WHERE path_count < 2
condition: Host has fewer than 2 I/O paths to the array
impact: Single point of failure for host I/O; if path fails, host loses access to all volumes; no path failover capability; cannot perform non-disruptive controller maintenance
remediation_steps: 1. SANtricity System Manager > Storage > Hosts > select host > view port connections. 2. Verify host HBA/initiator configuration — should have at least 2 ports connected. 3. Ensure each host HBA connects to a different controller for redundancy. 4. Install and configure multipath software on the host (DM-MPIO for Windows, multipathd for Linux). 5. Verify multipath policy on host matches recommended settings for E-Series. 6. Test path failover by temporarily disabling one path. 7. For FC: ensure zoning allows host to see both controllers. 8. For iSCSI: configure multiple sessions from different subnets if possible
support_case_note: Open support case for multipath configuration assistance or for host compatibility verification

name: SNMP or Alert Notifications Not Configured
description: Neither email nor SNMP alert notifications are configured for the E-Series array
check_query: SELECT array_name, email_configured, snmp_configured, syslog_configured FROM eseries_alert_config WHERE email_configured = false AND snmp_configured = false
condition: No alert notification method (email or SNMP) is configured
impact: Critical hardware events may go unnoticed; reliance on manual SANtricity monitoring; delayed incident response; increased risk of data loss from undetected failures
remediation_steps: 1. SANtricity System Manager > Settings > Alerts. 2. Configure email notifications: add SMTP server and recipient addresses. 3. Configure SNMP traps: add SNMP community/credentials and trap destination. 4. Send test alerts to verify delivery. 5. Configure syslog forwarding if available. 6. Set alert levels — ensure critical and warning events generate notifications. 7. Consider integrating with SANtricity Unified Manager for centralized alerting across multiple arrays
support_case_note: Not required. Open support case if alert delivery is inconsistent despite correct configuration

name: MEL Events Require Attention
description: Major Event Log (MEL) contains critical or needs-attention events that have not been acknowledged
check_query: SELECT array_name, event_count, critical_count, needs_attention_count, oldest_unacknowledged FROM eseries_mel_events WHERE needs_attention_count > 0 OR critical_count > 0
condition: MEL contains unacknowledged critical or needs-attention events
impact: Potential hardware issues being ignored; root cause of intermittent problems may be in MEL events; audit trail of array health not being reviewed
remediation_steps: 1. SANtricity System Manager > Support > Event Log. 2. Review all critical and needs-attention events. 3. Filter by severity and time range to prioritize. 4. Investigate and remediate root cause of each critical event. 5. Acknowledge events after investigation: mark as reviewed. 6. Set up regular MEL review schedule (recommend weekly for production arrays). 7. Export MEL for trend analysis and problem management. 8. If events indicate recurring hardware issues, open support case with MEL export
support_case_note: Open support case with MEL export attached if events indicate hardware issues requiring NetApp investigation
